note: IURC 'Cause' numbers are the case IDs under which utilities file. Search any cause number at the IURC portal to read the underlying filings. Ratepayer cost figures are as characterized by the Citizens Action Coalition and news reporting; utilities frequently redact detail.

dockets.title: AES Indiana large-load agreement (Google Monrovia)
dockets.ratepayer_note: AES claims the deal saves customers $770M over 15 years; Google pays 100% of its power + new infrastructure. CAC contests the savings framing.

dockets.title: Duke Energy TDSIC-5 (incl. Meta Jeffersonville upgrades)
dockets.ratepayer_note: $216M in network upgrades tied to Meta shifted to all Indiana customers (per CAC). Note: the IURC portal lists Cause 45647 as Duke's TDSIC plan with only Duke and the OUCC as parties; the Meta-specific attribution of costs within it is not confirmed from the docket itself and is pending verification.

dockets.title: I&M Expedited Generation Resource Plan
dockets.ratepayer_note: Decided 2026-01-28. The IURC portal lists this as I&M's expedited generation resource plan, not an interconnection case, and Amazon Data Services is a party while Google is not — an earlier version of this record described it incorrectly. Dollar figures inside the order are unverified.

dockets.title: I&M large-load tariff (Industrial Power Tariff modifications)
dockets.ratepayer_note: Approved 2025-02-19. This is Indiana's large-load tariff: it applies to new or expanded facilities at 70 MW or more on a single site (150 MW aggregated), and a customer planning to cut more than 20% of contracted peak capacity must get IURC approval first. Cost-allocation method was deferred to later proceedings, so it does not by itself settle who pays for new infrastructure.

sources.label: Utility Dive — IURC large-load interconnection order

dockets.title: I&M coal contract cost recovery
dockets.ratepayer_note: Shifts uneconomic coal contract costs through 2040 onto Indiana customers.

dockets.title: NIPSCO / NIPSCO Generation — data-center special contract and PPA
dockets.ratepayer_note: Approved 2026-06-17. Serves 'Data Center Customer #1' (Amazon, per NiSource) through NIPSCO Generation LLC rather than from rate base, with roughly $7B of generation and transmission funded outside ratepayer costs and a projected credit to existing customers. The OUCC has appealed the related ring-fencing order in Cause 46183.
